metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the existing `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` test call-sites as consumers and specify whether the `rebar-version:` line is appended unconditionally (requiring test updates) or injected via a new parameter (preserving existing call-sites).
findings.finding: Existing unit tests that call `build_manifest` and assert its output (outside `attest.py`'s directory) are unenumerated consumers whose fixture manifests will gain an unexpected `rebar-version:` line. The plan says `compute_validity` is untouched but does not address these test files.
findings.location: What section / AC5: 'Unit tests: both manifests carry the line'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name and locate the `rebar-version:` prefix constant (e.g. `_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX = 'rebar-version:'`) and specify which module (signing.py or attest.py) owns it so both emitters and the parser share a single source of truth.
findings.finding: The prefix constant for the 'rebar-version:' manifest line (analogous to _REGVER_PREFIX in attest.py) has no specified name or module home. Both emitters (build_manifest in attest.py and _verdict_manifest in transition_close.py) and the parser (manifest_rebar_version in attest.py) must reference the same constant, but the plan never names it or says which module owns it — an implementer must invent the name and decide whether it lives in signing.py or attest.py, creating a divergence risk.
findings.location: What section — resolver spec; AC 'both manifests carry the line'; manifest_rebar_version() spec
findings.suggested_fix: Add `_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX = 'rebar-version:'` to `signing.py` alongside the resolver (where both `attest.py` and `transition_close.py` already import `signing`), so both emitters call `signing.gate_code_version_stamp()` and both use `signing._REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX` — mirroring the `verified_at_sha_step` pattern already in signing.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that the new resolver is NOT a direct call to `head_sha()` but a new subprocess that adds `--short` and uses `os.path.dirname(rebar.__file__)` as the `-C` argument, only reusing the try/OSError/returncode pattern from `head_sha()`.
findings.finding: The plan says the new resolver reuses 'the `head_sha()` idiom, line 337 — a `git -C … rev-parse` subprocess', but `head_sha()` calls `rev-parse HEAD` (full 40-char SHA), never `rev-parse --short HEAD`. The plan then prescribes `--short` for the new resolver. The subprocess _pattern_ is reusable, but the flag is new and the claim of idiomatic reuse could mislead an implementer into omitting `--short` or using `head_sha()` directly (which returns a full SHA, not a short one).
findings.location: What section — 'co-located with and reusing the `head_sha()` idiom, line 337 — a `git -C … rev-parse` subprocess'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief design-doc entry (ADR snippet, CLAUDE.md section, or inline docstring contract) capturing the `rebar-version:` field format and its evolution rules (live-git-first, omit-on-failure, build-baked fallback in story 2) so story 2/3 implementors have a canonical reference.
findings.finding: No ADR or design-doc update is planned for the new `rebar-version:` manifest field, which establishes a new cross-manifest contract (format, parser, future consumers in stories 2 and 3) that will need to be maintained and evolved.
findings.location: ## Scope / ## What — no documentation step mentioned
findings.suggested_fix: Add a docstring to `manifest_rebar_version()` in `attest.py` that documents the canonical format, its optional SHA component, and the pre-stamp `None` contract; reference this docstring from the resolver in `signing.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan: state whether old code gracefully ignores unknown manifest lines (and how), or specify an expand-contract sequencing step (e.g., parser tolerates but ignores the new line before stamping is enabled).
findings.finding: The plan adds a `rebar-version:` line to produced manifests (a schema change) but provides no explicit rollback/back-out path if the story must be reverted after deployment. Old code encountering new manifests with the extra line is not addressed.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' sub-section to Scope: (1) confirm that compute_validity and verify_signature ignore unrecognised lines by design (cite the parsing logic), so rolled-back code continues to validate new-format manifests; or (2) if they do NOT ignore unknown lines, add an expand-contract step — ship the tolerant parser first, then enable stamping.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether the new gate-code SHA resolver (and existing `head_sha()`) should use `run_git()` from `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py` rather than a raw `subprocess.run`, given that `run_git()` was introduced to consolidate exactly this pattern; if `signing.py` is intentionally kept free of `_store` imports (e.g. for import-order reasons), document that exception in the plan.
findings.finding: The plan instructs implementing the gate-code SHA resolver with a raw `subprocess.run` (co-locating with `head_sha()` in signing.py) rather than using the existing `run_git()` wrapper in `src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py`, which was introduced specifically to consolidate the 'dozen hand-rolled git() wrappers' that had drifted into inconsistent shapes. The new resolver would add yet another raw subprocess call outside that wrapper.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section — resolver spec; src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py; src/rebar/signing.py:344-354

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `run_git` (`src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py`) should be used for the new gate-code SHA resolver's git calls, or explicitly justify why `head_sha()`'s raw `subprocess.run` pattern (which predates `run_git`) is intentionally preserved in `signing.py` rather than migrated.
findings.finding: The plan proposes a new inline `subprocess.run` git invocation in `signing.py` without considering or dismissing `run_git` (`src/rebar/_store/gitutil.py`), the established consolidation wrapper created specifically to eliminate hand-rolled git subprocesses. The plan's 'do NOT invent a new git wrapper' instruction is ambiguous: it likely means 'don't write a new wrapper function', but is easily read as 'don't use run_git either', which would produce a 13th bespoke subprocess call despite the prior-art consolidation.
findings.location: What section — 'a `git -C … rev-parse` subprocess; do NOT invent a new git wrapper'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'do NOT invent a new git wrapper' with 'use `run_git` from `rebar._store.gitutil` for the subprocess calls (two calls: `rev-parse --short HEAD` and `status --porcelain`), mirroring how other commands in `_commands/` and `_store/` use it; note that `head_sha()` itself is a pre-existing exception that will be migrated separately.' This makes the intent unambiguous and keeps the new code consistent with the consolidation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a short timeout (e.g., 2–5 seconds) on both subprocess calls in the resolver, treating timeout expiry as a git failure (omit SHA, emit debug log), to match the silent-failure contract and prevent indefinite blocking.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify a timeout on the two new subprocess calls (`git rev-parse` and `git status --porcelain`), creating a potential hang in the manifest-signing path if git blocks (e.g., network-mounted repo, git lock contention).
findings.suggested_fix: Pass `timeout=5` to both `subprocess.run()` calls and catch `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` alongside other exceptions in the git-failure handler.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all existing consumers of `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` text (beyond `compute_validity`) and confirm each is tolerant of the appended `rebar-version:` line, or explicitly note that no other consumers exist.
findings.finding: The plan mutates the output format of `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` but only explicitly clears `compute_validity` as a tolerant consumer; other callers that read or display these manifests (e.g., `verify_signature`, any `show`-related code) are not enumerated or confirmed tolerant of the new appended line.
findings.location: ## What — 'Append one rebar-version: line to BOTH manifests' + AC #4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence in ## Scope or ## What: 'Audit of existing manifest consumers: only `compute_validity` reads the manifest body; `verify_signature` delegates to `compute_validity` and does not independently parse lines — no other callers require changes.' If other consumers exist, list them and assert tolerance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the file reference for `_verdict_manifest`: it lives in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (defined at line 161), NOT in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `_verdict_manifest` lives in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but that is false: `_verdict_manifest` is defined in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` at line 161. Line 282 of `transition.py` is a comment block about the close path that delegates to `transition_close`. The story's implementation work must target `transition_close.py`, not `transition.py`.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '`_verdict_manifest` — `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`' with '`_verdict_manifest` — `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`' in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_verdict_manifest` location in the plan from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`.
findings.finding: `_verdict_manifest` is attributed to the wrong file and line number. The plan says it lives at `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but `_verdict_manifest` is defined at `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`. Line 282 of `transition.py` is a comment block delegating to `close_ticket`. An implementor following the plan verbatim will look in the wrong module.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_verdict_manifest` file pointer in the plan from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (the actual definition site at ~line 161).
findings.finding: The plan directs the implementer to modify `_verdict_manifest` at `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but that function is actually defined at `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (line 161). An implementer following the plan's pointer will open the wrong file, find no such function there, and must guess where to look.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `_verdict_manifest — src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` with `_verdict_manifest — src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (defined at ~line 161; imported and called from transition.py via close_ticket).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `manifest_rebar_version()` lives in `attest.py` (plan-review only) or in `signing.py` (shared, accessible to both plan-review and completion-verifier consumers), and if attest.py, add an explicit note that transition_close.py consumers must import it from there.
findings.finding: manifest_rebar_version() is placed in attest.py (a plan-review module), but _verdict_manifest in transition_close.py also emits the rebar-version: line. The plan never specifies how a caller parses the rebar-version stamp from a completion-verifier manifest — there is no specified import path from transition_close.py's manifest consumer to attest.py's parser, and importing plan-review machinery into a completion-verifier consumer would be a structural cross-dependency the plan does not acknowledge.
findings.location: What section — 'Add manifest_rebar_version(manifest) in attest.py'; '_verdict_manifest — src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
